title: Four By The Clock.
content:
"NAHANT, September 8, 1880,
    Four o'clock in the morning."

    Four by the clock! and yet not day;
    But the great world rolls and wheels away,
    With its cities on land, and its ships at sea,
    Into the dawn that is to be!

    Only the lamp in the anchored bark
    Sends its glimmer across the dark,
    And the heavy breathing of the sea
    Is the only sound that comes to me.
meaning:
"Four By The Clock." is a quintessential example of Henry Wadsworth Longfellow's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
